# Contributing to FastKit Core

Thank you for your interest in contributing! FastKit Core is built by developers, for developers — and every contribution matters, whether it's a bug fix, a new feature, or improving documentation.

## Full Contributing Guide

The complete contributing guide lives at **[fastkit.org/contribute](https://fastkit.org/contribute)** and covers:

- Setting up your local development environment
- Project structure and architecture overview
- Coding standards and conventions
- Writing and running tests
- Submitting pull requests

## Quick Start

```bash
# Fork and clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/fastkit-core.git
cd fastkit-core

# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Run tests
uv run pytest

# Run linting
uv run ruff check fastkit_core/
```

## Ways to Contribute

**Not sure where to start?** Look for issues labeled [`good first issue`](https://github.com/fastkit-org/fastkit-core/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) — these are well-defined tasks with clear acceptance criteria and maintainer support.

Other ways to help:

- **Report bugs** — use the [bug report template](https://github.com/fastkit-org/fastkit-core/issues/new?template=bug_report.md)
- **Suggest features** — use the [feature request template](https://github.com/fastkit-org/fastkit-core/issues/new?template=feature_request.md)
- **Improve documentation** — fix typos, add examples, clarify explanations
- **Write tests** — help us get closer to 100% coverage
- **Share FastKit** — write about it, star the repo, tell your team

## Code of Conduct

Be respectful and constructive. We're all here to build something useful together.

## Questions?

Open a [GitHub Discussion](https://github.com/fastkit-org/fastkit-core/discussions) or reach out at [hello@fastkit.org](mailto:hello@fastkit.org).
